title: Python Data Science Handbook
author: Jake VanderPlas
summary: The definitive guide to the Python data science stack — NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Scikit-Learn, and IPython covered comprehensively.
thoughts: "Best technical reference I've found. Dense but worth every page."

title: Think Python
author: Allen B. Downey
summary: An introduction to Python with a computer science mindset. Takes a problem-solving approach to teach programming concepts incrementally.
thoughts: "Perfect for beginners. The exercises are well-crafted and explanations are crisp."

title: Intro to Data Science
author: Joel Grus
summary: Introduction to data science covering the full pipeline — collection, cleaning, analysis, visualization, and intro to machine learning.
thoughts: "Currently working through it. Chapters 2 and 3 are excellent."

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
summary: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones using small 1% improvements compounded over time.
thoughts: "Changed how I think about systems vs goals. Identity-based habits are the key insight."

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Hunt & Thomas
summary: Classic software engineering book covering practical approaches — code quality, automation, and career advice for developers.
thoughts: "On my reading list. DRY and orthogonality principles alone make it worth reading."

title: Statistics in Plain English
author: Timothy Urdan
summary: Statistics explained without heavy mathematical notation — a solid bridge between intuition and formal statistical reasoning.
thoughts: "Queued up next. Need to solidify the stats foundation before going deeper into ML."
